requirements.part_name: Adjustable formwork corner alignment clamp - single metal part

material.material: 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized
material.reason: durable wear surface for workshop loading

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Adjustable formwork corner alignment clamp'. The
broader use case is: A reusable clamp used by concrete crews to hold intersecting form panels square
while bracing is installed. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: L-shaped clamp
body with sliding jaw blocks, screw bosses, ribbed webs, and panel-contact pads. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Locks against two panel faces and provides a visible reference corner for checking alignment
before pouring. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests perpendicular
fixture geometry, sliding components, clamping pads, reinforcement ribs, and readable functional
contact surfaces. The part is made from 1045 medium-carbon steel, normalized using sheet or plate
stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots,
pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in
the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

requirements.part_name: Concrete edge screed rail bracket - single metal part

material.material: AISI 316 stainless steel
material.reason: corrosion resistance and cleanable metal surfaces

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Concrete edge screed rail bracket'. The broader use
case is: A jobsite bracket used to support temporary screed rails along slab edges and pour
boundaries. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Offset bracket with rail
cradle, vertical adjustment slot, locking knob boss, and clamp foot. All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds
a straight rail at an adjustable elevation while staying anchored to surrounding formwork. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests offset load paths, slot-based
adjustment, rail cradles, and the relationship between temporary support and reference surface. The
part is made from AISI 316 stainless steel using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round
metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and
radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 48 mm and length 90 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 9 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 19 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 16 mm wide over 82 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=30 mm and X=60 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 3 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 5 mm wide across the top flat at X=45 mm, depth 4 mm.
